name: Shanghai
intro: Shanghai is one of China's most international and traveler-friendly big cities, combining a world-famous skyline, elegant historic districts, excellent food, and easy short itineraries that still feel rich and varied.
seoTitle: Shanghai Travel Guide for First-Time Visitors
seoDescription: Plan a first trip to Shanghai with practical advice on neighborhoods, airport transfers, where to stay, skyline districts, food, and easy short itineraries.
idealFor: travelers who want modern China with comfort and variety
tripPace: Works best as a compact but high-density city break with room for skyline, neighborhoods, food, shopping, and evenings.
highlights: One of the clearest places to experience modern urban China at full scale, from the Bund and Lujiazui skyline to polished shopping streets and late-night neighborhoods.
A comfortable starting city for travelers who want international flights, modern transport, manageable navigation, and a softer landing into China without giving up excitement.
Supports short itineraries unusually well because skyline districts, former-concession streets, food neighborhoods, museums, and riverfront evenings can be grouped cleanly.
Easy to combine with nearby scenic or slower-paced extensions like Hangzhou, while still being strong enough to stand on its own as a rewarding city stop.

planningPriorities.title: Treat Shanghai as a layered global city
planningPriorities.description: Mix skyline stops with neighborhoods, food streets, architecture, shopping, and flexible walking time instead of reducing the city to only a skyline checklist.

planningPriorities.title: Choose the right neighborhood base
planningPriorities.description: The right hotel area matters more than chasing one famous address because Shanghai is best experienced by district.

planningPriorities.title: Plan nearby extensions early
planningPriorities.description: If Hangzhou or another nearby city is part of the trip, shape the rail timing before finalizing the city days.

transportNote: Shanghai is one of the easiest first cities in China for international travelers because airport transfers, metro use, food access, and day-to-day movement are comparatively straightforward for a city of this scale.
heroImageAlt: Pudong skyline in Shanghai with the Oriental Pearl Tower and riverfront towers.
